CONFIG

  The spx config command can create, query, or delete SPX
  configuration data stored in SPX configuration datastore files.

USAGE: spx config [HIVE] [COMMAND] [@FILE] [...]

  --set [NAME=]VALUE            (see: spx help config set)
  --clear NAME                  (see: spx help config clear)

  spx config @key --set 436172626F6E20697320636F6F6C2121

  spx help config advanced
  spx help config examples

SPX - Azure Speech CLI, Version 1.48.2+f989a67ba897ceb06ceda0190c5623752bb62d93
Copyright (c) 2022 Microsoft Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

INIT

  The spx init command allows interactive and non-interactive selection
  or creation of Azure Speech Services resources.

  When a Speech resource is selected or created using this command, the
  associated resource keys and region are retrieved and automatically
  stored in the local SPX configuration datastore.

USAGE: spx init [...]

  AZURE
    --subscription SUBSCRIPTION   (see: spx help init subscription)

  INTERACTIVE
    --interactive BOOL            (see: spx help init interactive)

  RESOURCE
    --region region               (see: spx help init region)
    --group GROUP                 (see: spx help init group)
    --name NAME                   (see: spx help init name)

EXAMPLES

  EXAMPLE 1: interactively select or create an Azure Speech resource

    spx init

  EXAMPLE 2: interactively select a resource matching a set of criteria

    spx init --name "speech-us" --region westus2

  EXAMPLE 3: non-interactively select or create a resource by specifying all parameters

    spx init --interactive false --subscription "Pay-As-You-Go" --region westus2 --group "speech-4372626e-rg" --name "speech-4372626e"

SEE ALSO

  spx help setup
  spx help config
  spx help config hive
  spx help init advanced
  spx help init examples
  spx help find topics init
